note: If multiple YAML patterns match the same lipid class, the highest internal score is selected, following the .score_rule logic implemented in the parser.
For classes with split='auto', chain parsing tries '/' first, then '_', then whitespace, and otherwise leaves the remaining string unsplit.
When ignore_sum_only=TRUE, names with only one chain token and without '/', '_', FA suffixes, or tail FA annotations are treated as sum-composition-only and no chain metadata are returned.
If a lipid name contains 'A|B', the parser keeps the alternative containing the largest number of chain-like tokens.
Reported chain tokens are normalized by removing FA, O-, and P- prefixes; converting ';2O' to ';O2'; converting '18:1(2OH)' to '18:1;(2OH)'; and discarding 0:0 placeholder chains.
For classes with exclude_first_chain=TRUE, the first chain-like token is removed only when two or more chain tokens are present.
detect_fa_suffix captures chains encoded in parentheses, while detect_fa_tail captures trailing 'FA xx:y' annotations outside parentheses.
